address.description: Optional server of the chat application. If no network is specified, the address is assumed to be reachable on the public Internet.

chat-id.description: Identifier of the chat, room, channel, guild, or conversation containing the message.

forwarded-from-chat-id.description: Identifier of the original chat or conversation from which the message was forwarded.

forwarded-from-message-id.description: Identifier of the original forwarded message.

network.description: Optional network used to access the chat application, such as Tor or I2P. It is only used when the service is not reachable on the public Internet.

protocol.description: Name of the chat protocol or application, such as matrix, xmpp, or telegram.

protocol-instance-uuid.description: UUIDv5 identifier of the chat application, generated from the protocol name and, when specified, the network and address.

reaction.description: Reaction or emoji attached to the message.

reply-to-channel-id.description: Identifier of the channel or conversation containing the replied-to message.

reply-to-message-id.description: Identifier of the message this message replies to.

subchannel-id.description: Identifier of a subchannel or subchat.

thread-id.description: Identifier of the discussion thread containing the message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the message was created or sent.

url.description: Permalink or external URL pointing to the message, if available.

user-account-id.description: Identifier of the user account that authored or posted the message.

description: A message exchanged on a chat or messaging platform
name: chat-message
